title: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
author: Aaron Hoffman
orientation: Aaron Hoffman recounts the Oklahoma Senate Health and Human Services committee's unanimous Feb. 3, 2021 vote killing SB495, the Equal Protection and Equal Justice Act, and indicts pro-life Republican leadership for backroom maneuvering against abolition. He calls Oklahoma Christians to humble self-examination, repentance, and aggressive activism leading into Abolition Day.
audiences: National observer of state-level abolition politics

questions.question: What bill was killed in the Oklahoma Senate HHS committee on Feb. 3, 2021, and what would it have done?
questions.audience: National observer of state-level abolition politics
questions.answer: Senator Warren Hamilton presented SB495, the Equal Protection and Equal Justice Act, which would have criminalized abortion as murder and extended equal protection to all preborn human beings in Oklahoma. All ten committee members voted against it.
questions.quote: Oklahoma State Senator Warren Hamilton of District 7 presented the Equal Protection and Equal Justice Act (SB495) to the Senate Health and Human Services committee. This bill would’ve criminalized abortion as murder and provided equal protection to all preborn human beings in the state of Oklahoma.

questions.question: How did the seven Republicans on the committee vote, and why does Hoffman find that significant?
questions.answer: All seven Republicans, alongside the three Democrats, voted no. Hoffman highlights this to puncture the assumption that pro-life Republican majorities will reliably advance abolition legislation.
questions.quote: You might think, “Well of course this is going to pass! Seven pro-life Republicans will vote yes and it’ll pass!” Sadly, you would be wrong. Every single one of the senators listed above voted against the bill.

questions.question: What happened to SB13, the nearly identical bill from 2020?
questions.audience: National observer of state-level abolition politics
questions.answer: SB13 was filed by former Senator Joseph Silk and ignored by the same committee under chairman Greg McCortney, who refused to grant it a hearing. It died without ever being heard.
questions.quote: In Feb. 2020, a nearly identical bill, Senate Bill 13, the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act filed by former State Senator Joseph Silk, was simply ignored by the very same committee. It died without ever even being heard. Republican pro-life Senator Greg McCortney, chairman of the Health and Human Services Committee, refused to allow the bill a hearing.

questions.question: What does Hoffman allege about the role of Senate leadership in suppressing abolition bills?
questions.answer: He alleges that Greg Treat and Kim David, the most powerful pro-life Republicans in the chamber, have used backroom dealings and pressure on rank-and-file Republicans to stifle abolition legislation, and that Kim David personally whipped votes against SB13 on the Senate floor.
questions.quote: It is very apparent that Senate leadership is doing everything in their power to stifle this bill and the Abolitionist Movement by means of backroom dealings and pressuring the rest of the Republican Senators.

questions.question: What consequence did Joseph Silk reportedly face for advocating abolition?
questions.answer: Silk was stripped of his vice chairmanship in 2019 because of his advocacy for abolition, illustrating the cost imposed on legislators who break from leadership on this issue.
questions.quote: Joseph Silk was stripped of his vice chairmanship in 2019 for his advocacy for abolition.

questions.question: Why does Hoffman say the bill was killed so swiftly the week before Abolition Day?
questions.answer: He argues leadership timed the vote deliberately to deflate thousands of Christians planning to lobby at the Capitol, attempting to dampen morale rather than face sustained pressure.
questions.quote: Killing the bill this swiftly, the week before Abolition Day when they know that thousands of Christians will be showing up to lobby for the bill, is clearly an attempt to dampen the spirits of those seeking to establish justice for the preborn in the state of Oklahoma.

questions.question: How does Hoffman use Luke 18's parable of the Pharisee and tax collector to confront Oklahoma Christians?
questions.answer: He recasts the Pharisee's prayer as Oklahoma's self-congratulation about being more righteous than states like California or New York, then urges readers instead to take the tax collector's posture of humble repentance.
questions.quote: “God, I thank you that we are not like other States. We are not like the corrupt, unjust, states like California and New York. We vote for political leaders who believe in God’s Word and establish justice accordingly. We are conservative, Republican, pro-life, and would never oppose a law to protect our preborn neighbors.”

questions.question: What share of guilt does Hoffman assign to ordinary Oklahomans?
questions.answer: He says voters bear immense guilt for electing, supporting, and funding leaders who proved unworthy of the responsibility entrusted to them, calling for self-examination rather than blame shifted entirely onto politicians.
questions.quote: It is also just as clearly evident that WE, the people of Oklahoma, bear an immense guilt for having elected these leaders, supported them, voted for them, and given money to their campaign efforts, believing them to be worthy of this responsibility when they are not.

questions.question: What scriptural basis does Hoffman cite for publicly exposing the committee's actions?
questions.answer: He cites Ephesians 5:11 and Luke 12:1-3, arguing Christians must not merely abstain from unfruitful works of darkness but actively expose them, since whatever is whispered in private will be proclaimed on the housetops.
questions.quote: Not only are we to take no part in the unfruitful works of darkness, but we are to instead expose them (Eph 5:11). This shameful, sinful action taken by the members of the Health and Human Services committee must be exposed.

questions.question: What concrete action does Hoffman recommend regarding existing bills filed under titles 12, 21, and 63?
questions.answer: He urges constituents to ask their House Rep and Senator whether they have any bill, in committee or already passed out of committee, under titles 12, 21, or 63, and to request the language be replaced with the exact text of SB495.
questions.quote: Senate Bill 495 was filed under title 12, 21, and 63. Ask your House Rep and State Senator if they have a bill, either in committee or even one that has already passed in committee with any of the titles 12, 21, or 63, and request they completely change the language in the bill to the exact language that was in Senate Bill 495.

questions.question: Why does Hoffman insist the Abolition Day rally must be large despite the SB495 vote?
questions.answer: Because the legislature gauges crowd size to decide whether passing a bill is politically necessary, a massive turnout signals that opposing abolition will cost re-election, countering leadership's plan to demoralize abolitionists.
questions.quote: This rally is extremely important, because the legislature evaluates the size of the crowd to determine whether it is important for them to pass our bill or not, and how likely they are to lose re-election if they oppose the abolition bill.

questions.question: What does Hoffman argue about the authority of Christ over civil government?
questions.answer: He argues Christ in the Great Commission claimed all authority in heaven and on earth, so Oklahoma's state leaders who refuse to abolish abortion are living in active rebellion against the Lord Jesus Christ.
questions.quote: The Lord Jesus declared victory, power, and rule,  possessing ALL authority in heaven AND on earth, and as such, our state leaders are living in rebellion against the authority of the Lord Jesus Christ.

related.title: BREAKING: SB495 Might be Heard in Senate HHS Committee on Feb. 3!
related.reason: Sets up the very committee hearing whose disastrous outcome Hoffman dissects in this article.

related.title: OK Senate HHS Committee Votes in Favor of Murdering 10,000 Babies
related.reason: James Silberman's news report on the same Feb. 3 vote, naming each of the ten senators Hoffman lists.

related.title: Pastor Sam Ketcher: Repent With Us and Come to Abolition Day
related.reason: Frames the Abolition Day rally Hoffman calls Christians to attend in massive numbers despite the SB495 defeat.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Documents the 2020 SB13 obstruction by HHS chairman Greg McCortney that Hoffman recounts as the precursor to the SB495 killing.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Profiles the Senate Pro Tem Hoffman blames for backroom pressure and tells readers to flood with calls and emails.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Earlier indictment of the same Republican leadership network (including Treat) Hoffman names as the architects of SB495's defeat.

related.title: Letter to Oklahoma Senators From a Post-Abortive Mother
related.reason: Companion appeal addressed to the same senators whose SB495 vote Hoffman is asking constituents to confront.
